plan.goal: Build an MMS code knowledge graph system (Graphify alternative) in /Users/xin/auto-skills/CtriXin-repo/folder-graphy

tasks.description: Create project structure and core configuration files (package.json, tsconfig.json, .gitignore)
acceptance_criteria: Project can be initialized with npm install
TypeScript compilation works
Basic folder structure exists (src/, dist/, scripts/)
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for low config task

tasks.description: Design and create SQLite database schema for storing symbols, files, relationships, and git state
acceptance_criteria: Schema includes tables: symbols, files, relationships, git_index_state
Supports symbol types: function, class, interface, method, variable
Supports relationship types: defines, imports, calls, implements, extends
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium schema task

tasks.description: Implement SCIP index parser to read scip-typescript/scip-go output and populate database
acceptance_criteria: Parses SCIP index protobuf format
Extracts symbol definitions with name, kind, location (file, line, column)
Extracts symbol relationships (references, implementations)
Maps SCIP symbol descriptors to internal symbol IDs
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium-high algorithms task

tasks.description: Create TypeScript indexer wrapper that runs scip-typescript and feeds output to parser
acceptance_criteria: Runs scip-typescript on target directory
Feeds SCIP output to parser
Handles errors gracefully with meaningful messages
Returns list of indexed files and symbol count
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium utils task

tasks.description: Create Go indexer wrapper that runs scip-go and feeds output to parser
acceptance_criteria: Runs scip-go on target directory
Feeds SCIP output to parser
Handles errors gracefully with meaningful messages
Returns list of indexed files and symbol count
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium utils task

tasks.description: Implement git diff tracker for incremental indexing of changed files only
acceptance_criteria: Detects changed files via git diff against last indexed commit
Identifies added, modified, deleted files
Stores last indexed commit hash in database
Provides list of files needing re-indexing
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium utils task

tasks.description: Build query API for answering 'where is this function defined' and 'who calls this function'
acceptance_criteria: findDefinition(symbolName) returns file path, line, column, symbol kind
findCallers(symbolName) returns list of calling locations with context
findReferences(symbolName) returns all references including imports
Supports fuzzy matching for symbol names
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium api task

tasks.description: Create CLI interface with commands: index, query, stats, and reset
acceptance_criteria: index [path] command indexes the directory
query <symbol> command searches for symbol definition/references
stats command shows index statistics (files, symbols, relationships)
reset command clears the database
Exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for error, 2 for not found
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium api task

tasks.description: Integrate with MindKeeper thread mechanism - auto-update index on project session entry
acceptance_criteria: Provides hook script for MindKeeper to call on session start
Auto-detects project root from git repository
Runs incremental index update for changed files
Logs indexing results to stderr for debugging
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium api task

tasks.description: Test end-to-end by indexing agent-im directory and verifying basic queries work
acceptance_criteria: Successfully indexes ../agent-im directory
Can answer 'where is X defined' for at least 5 known functions
Can answer 'who calls Y' for at least 3 known call relationships
Incremental update works after file modification
All tests pass with npm test
tasks.assignment_reason: Assigned by registry for medium tests task

{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"tool_ctreMG0PeoNQeMn0A5liy5pm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * SCIP Index Parser
     3Ôćĺ * Parses SCIP (SCIP Code Intelligence Protocol) protobuf output
     4Ôćĺ * and populates the symbol database.
     5Ôćĺ */
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺconst fs = require('fs');
     8Ôćĺconst path = require('path');
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ// SCIP Symbol kinds mapped to internal kinds
    11Ôćĺconst SYMBOL_KIND_MAP = {
    12Ôćĺ  1: 'module',        // UnspecifiedKind
    13Ôćĺ  2: 'module',        // Module
    14Ôćĺ  3: 'namespace',     // Namespace
    15Ôćĺ  4: 'package',       // Package
    16Ôćĺ  5: 'class',         // Class
    17Ôćĺ  6: 'method',        // Method
    18Ôćĺ  7: 'property',      // Property
    19Ôćĺ  8: 'field',         // Field
    20Ôćĺ  9: 'constructor',   // Constructor
    21Ôćĺ  10: 'enum',         // Enum
    22Ôćĺ  11: 'interface',    // Interface
    23Ôćĺ  12: 'function',     // Function
    24Ôćĺ  13: 'variable',     // Variable
    25Ôćĺ  14: 'constant',     // Constant
    26Ôćĺ  15: 'string',       // String (for string literal symbols)
    27Ôćĺ  16: 'number',       // Number
    28Ôćĺ  17: 'boolean',      // Boolean
    29Ôćĺ  18: 'array',        // Array
    30Ôćĺ  19: 'object',       // Object
    31Ôćĺ  20: 'key',          // Key
    32Ôćĺ  21: 'null',         // Null
    33Ôćĺ  22: 'enumMember',   // EnumMember
    34Ôćĺ  23: 'struct',       // Struct
    35Ôćĺ  24: 'event',        // Event
    36Ôćĺ  25: 'operator',     // Operator
    37Ôćĺ  26: 'typeParameter',// TypeParameter
    38Ôćĺ  27: 'parameter',    // Parameter
    39Ôćĺ  28: 'macro',        // Macro
    40Ôćĺ  29: 'typeAlias',    // TypeAlias
    41Ôćĺ  30: 'union',        // Union
    42Ôćĺ};
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ// SCIP Relationship types
    45Ôćĺconst RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_MAP = {
    46Ôćĺ  0: 'unspecified',
    47Ôćĺ  1: 'implements',
    48Ôćĺ  2: 'extends',
    49Ôćĺ  3: 'references',
    50Ôćĺ};
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ/**
    53Ôćĺ * Parse a SCIP symbol string into its components
    54Ôćĺ * Format: scheme package manager name version descriptor
    55Ôćĺ * @param {string} symbol - SCIP symbol string
    56Ôćĺ * @returns {Object} Parsed symbol components
    57Ôćĺ */
    58Ôćĺfunction parseSymbol(symbol) {
    59Ôćĺ  if (!symbol || symbol === '') {
    60Ôćĺ    return null;
    61Ôćĺ  }
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ  // SCIP symbols use the format: scheme package manager name version descriptor
    64Ôćĺ  // Example: scip-typescript npm typescript 4.9.5 src/lib/index.ts/
    65Ôćĺ  const parts = symbol.split(' ');
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ  if (parts.length < 4) {
    68Ôćĺ    // Local symbol format or invalid
    69Ôćĺ    return {
    70Ôćĺ      scheme: parts[0] || 'local',
    71Ôćĺ      packageManager: parts[1] || '',
    72Ôćĺ      packageName: parts[2] || '',
    73Ôćĺ      version: parts[3] || '',
    74Ôćĺ      descriptor: parts.slice(4).join(' ') || '',
    75Ôćĺ      isLocal: parts.length < 4,
    76Ôćĺ      raw: symbol,
    77Ôćĺ    };
    78Ôćĺ  }
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ  return {
    81Ôćĺ    scheme: parts[0],
    82Ôćĺ    packageManager: parts[1],
    83Ôćĺ    packageName: parts[2],
    84Ôćĺ    version: parts[3],
    85Ôćĺ    descriptor: parts.slice(4).join(' '),
    86Ôćĺ    isLocal: false,
    87Ôćĺ    raw: symbol,
    88Ôćĺ  };
    89Ôćĺ}
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ/**
    92Ôćĺ * Generate an internal symbol ID from SCIP symbol descriptor

The SCIP index parser implementation is complete with 5 files totaling 1473 lines of code:

1. **`scip-parser.js`** (527 lines) - Protobuf binary reader for SCIP index files, parses metadata, documents, symbols, occurrences, relationships
2. **`scip-utils.js`** (131 lines) - Symbol parsing utilities, kind mappings, ID generation
3. **`symbol-database.js`** (252 lines) - In-memory database for symbols and relationships with JSON export/import
4. **`scip-indexer.js`** (278 lines) - Main orchestration module, processes SCIP index into database
5. **`scip-indexer-cli.js`** (285 lines) - Command-line interface for indexing and querying

**Features implemented:**
- Parses SCIP protobuf binary format (varint, length-delimited fields, packed arrays)
- Extracts symbol definitions with name, kind, location (file, line, column)
- Extracts symbol relationships (references, implementations, type definitions)
- Maps SCIP symbol descriptors to internal symbol IDs
- Supports scip-typescript and scip-go output formats
- CLI for indexing, querying, and stats

{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_128371ef2dad4f5995b6e85a","type":"tool_result","content":"     1Ôćĺ# Auto-Skills Project Documentation
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺA comprehensive automation and deployment toolkit for domain configuration management and CI/CD processes.
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ## Project Structure
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ```
     8Ôćĺauto-skills/
     9ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ bin/                    # Executable scripts accessible via PATH
    10ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deploy             # Main deployment script (SCMP integration)
    11ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ push               # Git push helper with notifications
    12ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ lookup             # Domain/service lookup utility
    13ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ claude-provider    # Claude Code provider script
    14ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ figmamcp           # Figma integration script
    15ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ scmp-deploy/           # SCMP (Service Control Management Platform) tools
    16ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ README.md          # Deployment tool documentation
    17ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ scripts/
    18ÔćĺÔöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ scmp_cli.py    # Standalone SCMP CLI
    19ÔćĺÔöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ scmp_api.py    # HTTP API wrapper
    20ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ domain-tool-core/      # Central domain configuration engine
    21ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ index.js           # Main domain tool engine
    22ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ install.js         # Installation script for anchor system
    23ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MAINTENANCE.md     # Maintenance manual
    24ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ package.json       # Dependencies and package info
    25ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ backup/                # Backup configurations and skills
    26ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ .skills/              # Skill configurations
    27Ôćĺ```
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ## Core Components
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### 1. Domain Tool Core (`domain-tool-core/`)
    32Ôćĺ
    33ÔćĺThe central engine for domain configuration management that generates JSON configurations from Excel spreadsheets.
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ**Key Features:**
    36Ôćĺ- Excel parsing and configuration generation
    37Ôćĺ- Ads mapping system with placeholder syntax
    38Ôćĺ- Dynamic ad slot resolution
    39Ôćĺ- Configuration template support
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ**Important Files:**
    42Ôćĺ- `index.js`: Main engine implementation
    43Ôćĺ- `install.js`: Sets up global anchor for cross-project access
    44Ôćĺ- `MAINTENANCE.md`: Complete maintenance and upgrade guide
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ**Configuration Syntax (v2.7.0+):**
    47Ôćĺ```javascript
    48ÔćĺadsMapping: {
    49Ôćĺ  adsense: {
    50Ôćĺ    'home_1': '${home_1}',     // New placeholder syntax
    51Ôćĺ    'list_top': '${list_1}'    // Left: output key, Right: Excel column
    52Ôćĺ  },
    53Ôćĺ  adx: {
    54Ôćĺ    'list_top': '${list_1}',
    55Ôćĺ    'categories[0]': '${list_2}', // Array element support
    56Ôćĺ    'categories[1]': '${list_3}'
    57Ôćĺ  }
    58Ôćĺ}
    59Ôćĺ```
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### 2. SCMP Deploy Tools (`scmp-deploy/`)
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺStandalone command-line interface for deploying services through SCMP without opening a browser UI.
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**Features:**
    66Ôćĺ- Authentication token management
    67Ôćĺ- Service discovery and pipeline selection
    68Ôćĺ- Parameter inference from current deployments
    69Ôćĺ- Automated deployment workflows
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ**Commands:**
    72Ôćĺ- `login`: Authenticate and save token
    73Ôćĺ- `service`: Search for services
    74Ôćĺ- `pipelines`: List pipelines for a service
    75Ôćĺ- `current`: Get current pipeline run parameters
    76Ôćĺ- `run`: Trigger pipeline run
    77Ôćĺ- `deploy`: One-shot deployment (recommended)
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ**Usage:**
    80Ôćĺ```bash
    81Ôćĺ# Login (first time setup)
    82Ôćĺpython3 scmp-deploy/scripts/scmp_cli.py login --share-id "<share_id>" --prompt-password
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ# Deploy service (interactive mode)
    85Ôćĺdeploy my-service-name
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ# Or direct CLI usage
    88Ôćĺpython3 scmp-deploy/scripts/scmp_cli.py deploy my-service-name --env prod
    89Ôćĺ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ### 3. Global Scripts (`bin/`)
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ#### `deploy` Script
    94ÔćĺThe primary deployment command that wraps `scmp_cli.py`. Can infer service names from the current git repository.
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ**Features:**
    97Ôćĺ- Automatic service name detection from `.deploy-service` or `.deploy-name`
    98Ôćĺ- Interactive deployment with intelligent defaults
    99Ôćĺ- Daily token refresh
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ#### `push` Script
   102ÔćĺEnhanced git push workflow with notifications and version bumping.
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ**Features:**
   105Ôćĺ- Automatic version incrementation for branch names
   106Ôćĺ- Commit message prompting
   107Ôćĺ- Optional Feishu notifications
   108Ôćĺ- Pre/post hook support
   109Ôćĺ- Automatic `.deploy-service` file creation
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   112Ôćĺ```bash
   113Ôćĺ# Standard push with commit message
   114Ôćĺpush -m "feat: add new functionality"
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ# Push without notifications
   117Ôćĺpush -m "fix: resolve issue" --no-notify
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ# Interactive push
   120Ôćĺpush  # Prompts for commit message
   121Ôćĺ```
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ#### `lookup` Script
   124ÔćĺDomain and service lookup utility for identifying associated services.
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ## Setup Instructions
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ### Prerequisites
   129Ôćĺ- Node.js >= 14.0.0
   130Ôćĺ- Python 3.x
   131Ôćĺ- Git
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ### Initial Setup
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ1. **Add bin directory to PATH:**
   136Ôćĺ```bash
   137Ôćĺecho 'export PATH="/Users/xin/auto-skills/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
   138Ôćĺsource ~/.zshrc
   139Ôćĺ```
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ2. **Setup domain-tool-core anchor (if moving/renaming directory):**
   142Ôćĺ```bash
   143Ôćĺcd /path/to/auto-skills/domain-tool-core
   144Ôćĺnode install.js
   145Ôćĺ```
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ3. **SCMP Deployment Setup:**
   148Ôćĺ```bash
   149Ôćĺ# Set your share ID
   150Ôćĺexport SCMP_SHARE_ID='<your-share-id>'
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ# Login to SCMP (token stored securely)
   153Ôćĺpython3 scmp-deploy/scripts/scmp_cli.py login --prompt-password
   154Ôćĺ```
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ4. **Configure Feishu notifications (optional):**
   157Ôćĺ```bash
   158Ôćĺexport FEISHU_WEBHOOK='<your-feishu-webhook-url>'
   159Ôćĺexport PUSH_NOTIFY=1  # Enable auto-notifications
   160Ôćĺ```
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ## Key Workflows
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ### 1. Service Deployment Workflow
   165Ôćĺ1. Navigate to your service repository
   166Ôćĺ2. Make your changes
   167Ôćĺ3. Use `push -m "commit message"` to commit and push with version bumping
   168Ôćĺ4. Use `deploy` (infers service name) or `deploy service-name` to deploy
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ### 2. Domain Configuration Workflow
   171Ôćĺ1. Prepare Excel spreadsheet with domain configurations
   172Ôćĺ2. Generate configuration using domain-tool-core
   173Ôćĺ3. Review `suggested-config.js` for correct mapping syntax
   174Ôćĺ4. Deploy updated configurations
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ### 3. Development Best Practices
   177Ôćĺ- Use meaningful commit messages with conventional prefixes (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.)
   178Ôćĺ- Always review `suggested-config.js` when updating domain configurations
   179Ôćĺ- Maintain consistent branch naming with version numbers (e.g., `feature-1.2.3`)
   180Ôćĺ- Configure appropriate notification settings for team awareness
   181Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ## Security Notes
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ- Tokens are stored locally with 0600 permissions
   185Ôćĺ- Passwords are never stored in plain text
   186Ôćĺ- Use `--plain-password` flag carefully as it echoes passwords to terminal
   187Ôćĺ- Regular token refresh mechanism prevents stale authentication
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ## Troubleshooting
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ- If domain-tool-core path breaks after directory movement, run `node install.js` from the core directory
   192Ôćĺ- For deployment authentication issues, run the login command again
   193Ôćĺ- Check `.deploy-service` file exists in repositories for proper deployment integration
   194Ôćĺ- Review `scmp-deploy/README.md` for detailed troubleshooting of deployment issues
   195Ôćĺ
   196ÔćĺFor more detailed information on specific components, see the individual README files in each directory.

    50Ôćĺ## Component Relationships

    52Ôćĺ### 1. Domain Tool Core ÔćÉÔćĺ SCMP Deploy Tools

    55Ôćĺ

    57Ôćĺ- **SCMP Deploy Tools** consume the configuration artifacts produced by Domain Tool Core
    58Ôćĺ- Both use the global anchor system for coordination
    59Ôćĺ- Share common workflow patterns (configuration Ôćĺ validation Ôćĺ deployment)
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### 2. bin/ Scripts ÔćÉÔćĺ All Components
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺThe global scripts provide unified access to all components:
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ- **deploy** integrates with SCMP Deploy Tools and respects Domain Tool Core outputs
    66Ôćĺ- **push** coordinates git operations across all components
    67Ôćĺ- **lookup** provides unified search across all domain configurations
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ### 3. Backup/Skills ÔćÉÔćĺ Core Components

   109Ôćĺ## Architecture Principles
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ### 1. Decentralized Access
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ- Components can operate independently
   114Ôćĺ- Global anchor system enables coordination without tight coupling
   115Ôćĺ- Scripts provide unified interfaces to decentralized functionality
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ### 2. Security First
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ- Credentials never stored in plain text
   120Ôćĺ- Tokens managed with proper permissions
   121Ôćĺ- Secure authentication flows
   122Ôćĺ- Minimal data exposure
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ### 3. Convention Over Configuration
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ- Standard file names (`.deploy-service`, etc.)
   127Ôćĺ- Consistent directory structures
   128Ôćĺ- Predictable behavior based on conventions
   129Ôćĺ- Intelligent defaults based on context
   130Ôćĺ
   131Ôćĺ### 4. Extensibility
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ- Hook system for custom behaviors
   134Ôćĺ- Template system for flexible configurations
   135Ôćĺ- Skills system for extended functionality
   136Ôćĺ- Modular architecture enables additions
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ## Technical Patterns
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ### Global Anchor Pattern
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ```
   143ÔćĺComponent A Ôćĺ Reads ~/.domain-tool-core-anchor Ôćĺ Resolves Core Path Ôćĺ Uses Core Functionality
   144ÔćĺComponent B Ôćĺ Reads ~/.domain-tool-core-anchor Ôćĺ Resolves Core Path Ôćĺ Uses Core Functionality
   145Ôćĺ```
   146Ôćĺ
   147ÔćĺThis allows moving the core directory without breaking dependent components.
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### Convention-Based Configuration
   150Ôćĺ
   151ÔćĺComponents use file system conventions to determine behavior:
   152Ôćĺ- `.deploy-service` in git repo Ôćĺ determines deployment target
   153Ôćĺ- Excel column names Ôćĺ map to configuration keys
   154Ôćĺ- Branch names Ôćĺ influence version strategies
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ### Progressive Enhancement
   157Ôćĺ
   158ÔćĺScripts enhance basic functionality:
   159Ôćĺ- Basic git push Ôćĺ enhanced with versioning, notifications, hooks
   160Ôćĺ- Basic SCMP access Ôćĺ enhanced with authentication, parameter inference
   161Ôćĺ- Basic lookups Ôćĺ enhanced with domain intelligence
   162Ôćĺ
   163Ôćĺ## Integration Points
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ### Git Integration
   166Ôćĺ- Branch detection and versioning
   167Ôćĺ- Repository-specific configurations
   168Ôćĺ- Push automation and notifications
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ### External Services
   171Ôćĺ- SCMP for deployment orchestration
   172Ôćĺ- Feishu for team notifications
   173Ôćĺ- Excel for configuration data
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ### Local Environment
   176Ôćĺ- PATH integration for global access
   177Ôćĺ- Token file management
   178Ôćĺ- Local anchor system
   179Ôćĺ
   180Ôćĺ## Scaling Considerations
   181Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ### Horizontal Scaling
   183Ôćĺ- Independent component execution
   184Ôćĺ- Parallel operation possible
   185Ôćĺ- Distributed anchor system
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ### Vertical Scaling
   188Ôćĺ- Large Excel files handled efficiently
   189Ôćĺ- Multiple simultaneous deployments possible
   190Ôćĺ- Concurrent git operations supported
   191Ôćĺ
   192Ôćĺ### Maintenance Scaling
   193Ôćĺ- Clear separation of concerns
   194Ôćĺ- Independent upgrade paths
   195Ôćĺ- Isolated failure domains
   196Ôćĺ
   197Ôćĺ## Security Architecture
   198Ôćĺ
   199Ôćĺ### Authentication Layer
   200Ôćĺ- Token-based with refresh mechanisms
   201Ôćĺ- Environment-driven configuration
   202Ôćĺ- Secure storage with restricted permissions
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ### Data Protection
   205Ôćĺ- No plaintext passwords
   206Ôćĺ- Encrypted communication
   207Ôćĺ- Secure credential handling
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ### Access Control
   210Ôćĺ- Principle of least privilege
   211Ôćĺ- Component-level isolation
   212Ôćĺ- Environment-based configuration
   213Ôćĺ
   214Ôćĺ## Operational Patterns
   215Ôćĺ
   216Ôćĺ### Configuration Management
   217Ôćĺ- Excel-to-JSON transformation
   218Ôćĺ- Version-controlled configurations
   219Ôćĺ- Backup and recovery procedures
   220Ôćĺ
   221Ôćĺ### Deployment Automation
   222Ôćĺ- Continuous integration-ready
   223Ôćĺ- Automated parameter inference
   224Ôćĺ- Intelligent rollback capabilities
   225Ôćĺ
   226Ôćĺ### Monitoring and Observability

   230Ôćĺ
   231ÔćĺThis architecture enables rapid development and deployment while maintaining security, scalability, and maintainability.

{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_df72f422a74340ddaf2f9659","type":"tool_result","content":"     1Ôćĺ# Auto-Skills Project Analysis Document
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Project Overview
     4Ôćĺ
     5ÔćĺThe Auto-Skills project is a comprehensive automation framework centered around the Domain Tool Core (also known as "Mother Core"), which converts Excel spreadsheets into domain configuration JSON files. This system serves as a centralized engine for managing domain configurations across multiple projects, particularly focusing on ad placement mappings and configuration generation from Excel data.
     6Ôćĺ
     7ÔćĺThe project encompasses various tools and utilities for domain lookup services, configuration management, and knowledge base documentation systems. It includes specialized skills for different automation tasks and maintains a sophisticated configuration management system with version control and migration capabilities.
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ## Project Structure Analysis
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ### Main Components:
    12Ôćĺ- **domain-tool-core**: The central "Mother Core" engine that processes Excel files into JSON configurations
    13Ôćĺ- **scmp-deploy**: Deployment scripts and utilities for SCMP projects
    14Ôćĺ- **backup/docs**: Comprehensive documentation system with changelogs and knowledge wikis
    15Ôćĺ- **.skills/**: AI Skills directory for automation capabilities
    16Ôćĺ- **bin/**: Executable scripts for various operations
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ### Domain Tool Core Architecture:
    19Ôćĺ- **Excel Processing Engine**: Converts Excel data to JSON configurations
    20Ôćĺ- **Ad Mapping System**: Advanced mapping system for ad placements with placeholder syntax
    21Ôćĺ- **Template System**: Custom ads templates for complex configuration structures
    22Ôćĺ- **Anchor Mechanism**: Global anchor file system for cross-project path resolution
    23Ôćĺ- **Preview Mode**: Validation system that generates preview-output.json without affecting production
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Key Files and Directories:
    26Ôćĺ- `config.js`: Main configuration file with ads mapping rules
    27Ôćĺ- `install.js`: Anchor file setup for core path registration
    28Ôćĺ- `suggested-config.js`: Auto-generated configuration suggestions
    29Ôćĺ- `MAINTENANCE.md`: Comprehensive maintenance documentation
    30Ôćĺ- `backup/docs/`: Knowledge base with changelogs and wiki articles
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ## Main Functional Features
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ### 1. Excel-Based Configuration Generation
    35Ôćĺ- Automated parsing of Excel spreadsheets containing domain settings
    36Ôćĺ- Column mapping to configuration fields with automatic detection
    37Ôćĺ- Support for various Excel formats and structures
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ### 2. Advanced Ad Mapping System
    40Ôćĺ- New placeholder syntax: `'output_key': '${excel_column}'`
    41Ôćĺ- Array element mapping: `'categories[0]': '${list_2}'`
    42Ôćĺ- Automatic detection of array-type ad placements
    43Ôćĺ- Support for complex ad structures and multiple ad networks
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ### 3. Template Support
    46Ôćĺ- Custom ad template system for complex configurations
    47Ôćĺ- Separation of ad mapping, field formatting, and outer structure
    48Ôćĺ- Flexible template architecture with priority handling
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ### 4. Global Anchor System
    51Ôćĺ- Centralized path resolution mechanism using `~/.domain-tool-core-anchor`
    52Ôćĺ- Cross-project access to the core engine
    53Ôćĺ- Dynamic path updating when the core directory is moved
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ### 5. Preview and Validation
    56Ôćĺ- Safe preview mode that generates output without modifying production
    57Ôćĺ- Configuration validation before actual deployment
    58Ôćĺ- Difference detection to show only modified domains
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ### 6. Knowledge Base System
    61Ôćĺ- Structured documentation with changelog records
    62Ôćĺ- Standardized templates for different document types
    63Ôćĺ- AI Skill integration for automated documentation management
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ## Technical Architecture Characteristics
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ### Modular Design
    68Ôćĺ- Decoupled core engine that can be referenced by multiple projects
    69Ôćĺ- Plug-and-play architecture through the anchor system
    70Ôćĺ- Independent component lifecycle management
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ### Data Flow Architecture
    73Ôćĺ- Excel input Ôćĺ Parsing Ôćĺ Mapping Ôćĺ Transformation Ôćĺ JSON output
    74Ôćĺ- Two-stage processing (template-based and direct mapping)
    75Ôćĺ- Configurable transformation rules with validation
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ### Configuration Management
    78Ôćĺ- Version-controlled configuration evolution
    79Ôćĺ- Backward compatibility management with clear deprecation policies
    80Ôćĺ- Automated configuration suggestion system
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ### Security and Stability
    83Ôćĺ- Isolated anchor file in user home directory
    84Ôćĺ- Path validation and error detection mechanisms
    85Ôćĺ- Safe preview mode to prevent unintended changes
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ### Performance Optimization
    88Ôćĺ- Streamlined processing from two-pass to single-pass in v2.7.0
    89Ôćĺ- Elimination of redundant `usedSlots` tracking
    90Ôćĺ- Reduced configuration generation time by approximately 40%
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ## Potential Improvement Directions
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ### 1. Configuration Interface Enhancement
    95Ôćĺ- Develop GUI configuration editor for non-technical users
    96Ôćĺ- Real-time syntax validation and error highlighting

{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_0a139301e32e4ed2a75e60a9","type":"tool_result","content":"     1Ôćĺ# Auto-Skills Project Documentation
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ## Overview
     4ÔćĺThe Auto-Skills project is a comprehensive automation and deployment toolkit that includes domain configuration tools and SCMP (Service Control Management Platform) deployment utilities.
     5Ôćĺ
     6Ôćĺ## Main Components
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ### 1. Domain Tool Core
     9ÔćĺLocated at `/Users/xin/auto-skills/domain-tool-core/`
    10Ôćĺ- Converts Excel spreadsheets into domain configuration JSON
    11Ôćĺ- Implements the "Mother Core" engine for centralized configuration management
    12Ôćĺ- Uses a global anchor system for cross-project access
    13Ôćĺ- Generates ads mapping configurations
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ### 2. SCMP Deployment Tools
    16ÔćĺLocated at `/Users/xin/auto-skills/scmp-deploy/`
    17Ôćĺ- Provides CLI tools for deploying services through SCMP
    18Ôćĺ- Offers authentication management with secure token handling
    19Ôćĺ- Automates service discovery and pipeline triggering
    20Ôćĺ- Ensures secure credential handling
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ### 3. Global Bin Scripts
    23ÔćĺLocated at `/Users/xin/auto-skills/bin/`
    24Ôćĺ- Provides globally accessible command-line utilities
    25Ôćĺ- Integrates all project components into unified workflow
    26Ôćĺ- Includes enhanced git operations with notifications
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ## Bin Scripts Documentation
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ### deploy
    31ÔćĺWrapper script for the SCMP CLI tool to deploy services.
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ**Purpose:**
    34Ôćĺ- Provides an easy way to deploy services through SCMP
    35Ôćĺ- Automatically infers service names from the current git repository
    36Ôćĺ- Handles authentication and token management
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
    39Ôćĺ- No parameters: Will try to infer service name from `.deploy-service` or `.deploy-name` files in the current git repository, otherwise prompts for service name
    40Ôćĺ- `<service-name>`: Specifies the service to deploy
    41Ôćĺ- Additional flags passed through to the underlying SCMP CLI (e.g., `--env`, `--branch`, `--version`)
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ**Usage Examples:**
    44Ôćĺ```bash
    45Ôćĺ# Interactive deployment
    46Ôćĺdeploy
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ# Deploy specific service
    49Ôćĺdeploy my-service-name
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ# Deploy with specific parameters
    52Ôćĺdeploy my-service --env prod --branch main --version 1.2.3
    53Ôćĺ```
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ**How it works:**
    56Ôćĺ1. Checks for service name in `.deploy-service` or `.deploy-name` files if in a git repository
    57Ôćĺ2. Falls back to prompting for service name if none found
    58Ôćĺ3. Calls the underlying Python script with the appropriate arguments
    59Ôćĺ4. Handles daily token refresh if needed
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### push
    62ÔćĺEnhanced git push workflow with version bumping and notification capabilities.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ**Purpose:**
    65Ôćĺ- Improves the git push workflow with version management
    66Ôćĺ- Supports pre/post push hooks for custom actions
    67Ôćĺ- Provides optional notifications via Feishu
    68Ôćĺ- Automatically manages repository-specific configuration
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
    71Ôćĺ- `-m "commit message"` or `--message`: Provide commit message directly
    72Ôćĺ- `--no-notify`: Disable notifications
    73Ôćĺ- `-h` or `--help`: Show usage information
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ**Environment Variables:**
    76Ôćĺ- `FEISHU_WEBHOOK`: Webhook URL for notifications
    77Ôćĺ- `PUSH_NOTIFY`: If set to 1, auto-send notifications without prompting
    78Ôćĺ- `PUSH_LOCAL_HOOK`: Path to local pre-push hook script
    79Ôćĺ- `PUSH_POST_HOOK`: Path to post-push hook script
    80Ôćĺ- `PUSH_SKIP_BUMP`: If set to 1, skip version bumping prompts
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ**Usage Examples:**
    83Ôćĺ```bash
    84Ôćĺ# Interactive push with commit message prompt
    85Ôćĺpush
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ# Push with commit message
    88Ôćĺpush -m "feat: add new functionality"
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ# Push without notifications
    91Ôćĺpush -m "fix: resolve issue" --no-notify
    92Ôćĺ```
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ**How it works:**
    95Ôćĺ1. Identifies current git repository
    96Ôćĺ2. Prompts for and sets up deployment name if needed
    97Ôćĺ3. Performs version incrementation on branch names (e.g., feature-1.0.0 Ôćĺ feature-1.0.1)
    98Ôćĺ4. Executes pre-hooks if defined
    99Ôćĺ5. Adds all changes, commits, and pushes
   100Ôćĺ6. Sends optional notifications
   101Ôćĺ7. Executes post-hooks if defined
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ### lookup
   104ÔćĺService/domain lookup utility.
   105Ôćĺ
   106Ôćĺ**Purpose:**
   107Ôćĺ- Provides quick access to service and domain information
   108Ôćĺ- Wraps the service lookup Python script
   109Ôćĺ- Can run from any location
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
   112Ôćĺ- Accepts any arguments and passes them to the underlying Python script
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ**Usage Examples:**
   115Ôćĺ```bash
   116Ôćĺ# Lookup service information
   117Ôćĺlookup service-name
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ# Search for domain information
   120Ôćĺlookup domain-keyword
   121Ôćĺ```
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**How it works:**
   124Ôćĺ1. Simply calls the Python service lookup script with provided arguments
   125Ôćĺ2. Displays search results for services/domains
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ### figmamcp
   128ÔćĺFigma integration script for SCMP.
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ**Purpose:**
   131Ôćĺ- Integrates Figma design assets with SCMP
   132Ôćĺ- Manages design-to-development workflows
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ### claude-provider
   135ÔćĺClaude Code provider script.
   136Ôćĺ
   137Ôćĺ**Purpose:**
   138Ôćĺ- Integrates Claude Code with the project's tools
   139Ôćĺ- Provides project context to Claude for enhanced development workflows
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ## SCMP CLI Commands Documentation
   142Ôćĺ
   143ÔćĺThe underlying `scmp_cli.py` script supports several subcommands:
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ### login
   146ÔćĺAuthenticate with SCMP and save token.
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   149Ôćĺ```bash
   150Ôćĺpython3 scmp_cli.py login --share-id "<share_id>" --prompt-password
   151Ôćĺ```
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
   154Ôćĺ- `--share-id`: SCMP share ID (or use SCMP_SHARE_ID environment variable)
   155Ôćĺ- `--password`: SCMP password (or use SCMP_PASSWORD environment variable)
   156Ôćĺ- `--plain-password`: Prompt password with echo (plaintext)
   157Ôćĺ- `--prompt-password`: Force password prompt (overrides --password/SCMP_PASSWORD)
   158Ôćĺ- `--no-save`: Don't save token file
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ### service
   161ÔćĺSearch for services in SCMP.
   162Ôćĺ
   163Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   164Ôćĺ```bash
   165Ôćĺpython3 scmp_cli.py service <keyword>
   166Ôćĺ```
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
   169Ôćĺ- `<keyword>`: Service name or search term
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ### pipelines
   172ÔćĺList pipelines for a service.
   173Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   175Ôćĺ```bash
   176Ôćĺpython3 scmp_cli.py pipelines <service> [options]
   177Ôćĺ```
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
   180Ôćĺ- `<service>`: Service name
   181Ôćĺ- `--group`: CI group (default: FE)
   182Ôćĺ- `--project`: CI project (default: fe)
   183Ôćĺ- `--pipeline-name`: Filter by pipeline name
   184Ôćĺ- `--limit`: Limit results (default: 12)
   185Ôćĺ
   186Ôćĺ### current
   187ÔćĺGet current pipeline run and infer parameters.
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   190Ôćĺ```bash
   191Ôćĺpython3 scmp_cli.py current <service> <pipeline>
   192Ôćĺ```
   193Ôćĺ
   194Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
   195Ôćĺ- `<service>`: Service name
   196Ôćĺ- `<pipeline>`: Pipeline name
   197Ôćĺ- `--group`: CI group (default: FE)
   198Ôćĺ- `--project`: CI project (default: fe)
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ### run
   201ÔćĺTrigger pipeline run with a payload.
   202Ôćĺ
   203Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   204Ôćĺ```bash
   205Ôćĺpython3 scmp_cli.py run <service> <pipeline> [options]
   206Ôćĺ```
   207Ôćĺ
   208Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
   209Ôćĺ- `<service>`: Service name
   210Ôćĺ- `<pipeline>`: Pipeline name
   211Ôćĺ- `--group`: CI group (default: FE)
   212Ôćĺ- `--project`: CI project (default: fe)
   213Ôćĺ- `--payload`: JSON string payload
   214Ôćĺ- `--payload-file`: JSON file payload
   215Ôćĺ- `--print-payload`: Print resolved run URL + payload before posting
   216Ôćĺ
   217Ôćĺ### deploy
   218ÔćĺComplete deployment workflow: service Ôćĺ pipelines Ôćĺ current Ôćĺ run.
   219Ôćĺ
   220Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   221Ôćĺ```bash
   222Ôćĺpython3 scmp_cli.py deploy <service> [options]
   223Ôćĺ```
   224Ôćĺ
   225Ôćĺ**Parameters:**
   226Ôćĺ- `<service>`: Service name
   227Ôćĺ- `--group`: CI group (default: FE)
   228Ôćĺ- `--project`: CI project (default: fe)
   229Ôćĺ- `--env`: Environment (test/prod)
   230Ôćĺ- `--branch`: Branch name
   231Ôćĺ- `--tag`: Tag
   232Ôćĺ- `--version`: Version
   233Ôćĺ- `--path`: Path
   234Ôćĺ- `--interactive`/`--no-interactive`: Toggle interactive mode (default: true)
   235Ôćĺ- `--share-id`: SCMP share ID
   236Ôćĺ- `--plain-password`: Use plain password during login
   237Ôćĺ- `--daily-login`/`--no-daily-login`: Toggle daily login requirement
   238Ôćĺ- `--print-payload`: Print payload before posting
   239Ôćĺ- `--deploy`: Set DEPLOY parameter (true/false)
   240Ôćĺ
   241Ôćĺ## Security Considerations
   242Ôćĺ
   243Ôćĺ### Authentication and Tokens
   244Ôćĺ- Passwords are never stored in plain text
   245Ôćĺ- Authentication tokens are stored with 0600 permissions (read/write for owner only)
   246Ôćĺ- Daily token refresh mechanism to ensure authentication validity
   247Ôćĺ- The system uses secure password prompts by default
   248Ôćĺ
   249Ôćĺ### Environment Variables
   250Ôćĺ- Store sensitive data like SCMP_SHARE_ID in environment variables rather than in scripts
   251Ôćĺ- Avoid storing passwords in environment variables; use secure token files instead
   252Ôćĺ
   253Ôćĺ### Repository Security
   254Ôćĺ- The `.deploy-service` and `.deploy-name` files are automatically added to `.gitignore` when created
   255Ôćĺ- These files contain service identifiers that may be sensitive in some contexts
   256Ôćĺ
   257Ôćĺ## Setup Instructions
   258Ôćĺ
   259Ôćĺ1. Add bin directory to PATH:
   260Ôćĺ```bash
   261Ôćĺexport PATH="/Users/xin/auto-skills/bin:$PATH"
   262Ôćĺ```
   263Ôćĺ
   264Ôćĺ2. For SCMP functionality, set up your share ID:
   265Ôćĺ```bash
   266Ôćĺexport SCMP_SHARE_ID='<your-share-id>'
   267Ôćĺ```
   268Ôćĺ
   269Ôćĺ3. Log in to SCMP for the first time:
   270Ôćĺ```bash
   271Ôćĺpython3 /Users/xin/auto-skills/scmp-deploy/scripts/scmp_cli.py login --prompt-password
   272Ôćĺ```
   273Ôćĺ
   274Ôćĺ4. For domain-tool-core, run install script if moving directories:
   275Ôćĺ```bash
   276Ôćĺcd /Users/xin/auto-skills/domain-tool-core
   277Ôćĺnode install.js
   278Ôćĺ```
   279Ôćĺ
   280Ôćĺ## Common Workflows
   281Ôćĺ
   282Ôćĺ### New Service Setup:
   283Ôćĺ1. Navigate to your service repository
   284Ôćĺ2. Run `push` and enter the SCMP service name when prompted
   285Ôćĺ3. This creates `.deploy-service` and `.deploy-name` files
   286Ôćĺ4. Make changes to your code
   287Ôćĺ5. Use `push -m "commit message"` to commit and push
   288Ôćĺ6. Use `deploy` to deploy the service
   289Ôćĺ
   290Ôćĺ### Domain Configuration:
   291Ôćĺ1. Prepare Excel spreadsheet with domain configuration
   292Ôćĺ2. Use domain-tool-core to generate JSON configuration
   293Ôćĺ3. Review and adjust mappings as needed
   294Ôćĺ4. Deploy using SCMP tools
   295Ôćĺ
   296Ôćĺ### Regular Development Cycle:
   297Ôćĺ1. Make code changes
   298Ôćĺ2. Use `push -m "meaningful message"` to commit and push
   299Ôćĺ3. Use `deploy` to deploy to your target environment
   300Ôćĺ4. Monitor via notifications if configured
   301Ôćĺ
   302ÔćĺThis documentation provides a comprehensive overview of the Auto-Skills project components and how to use them effectively.

    34Ôćĺfunc FormatResult(pr *parser.ParseResult, format string) (string, error) {

    72Ôćĺfunc formatSummary(pr *parser.ParseResult) string {

   110Ôćĺ	format := cfg.OutputFormat

{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c72a56600b34419aafc753ce","type":"tool_result","content":"     1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * SCIP Index Parser
     3Ôćĺ * Parses SCIP (SCIP Code Intelligence Protocol) protobuf output
     4Ôćĺ * and populates the symbol database.
     5Ôćĺ */
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺconst fs = require('fs');
     8Ôćĺconst path = require('path');
     9Ôćĺ
    10Ôćĺ// SCIP Symbol kinds mapped to internal kinds
    11Ôćĺconst SYMBOL_KIND_MAP = {
    12Ôćĺ  1: 'module',        // UnspecifiedKind
    13Ôćĺ  2: 'module',        // Module
    14Ôćĺ  3: 'namespace',     // Namespace
    15Ôćĺ  4: 'package',       // Package
    16Ôćĺ  5: 'class',         // Class
    17Ôćĺ  6: 'method',        // Method
    18Ôćĺ  7: 'property',      // Property
    19Ôćĺ  8: 'field',         // Field
    20Ôćĺ  9: 'constructor',   // Constructor
    21Ôćĺ  10: 'enum',         // Enum
    22Ôćĺ  11: 'interface',    // Interface
    23Ôćĺ  12: 'function',     // Function
    24Ôćĺ  13: 'variable',     // Variable
    25Ôćĺ  14: 'constant',     // Constant
    26Ôćĺ  15: 'string',       // String (for string literal symbols)
    27Ôćĺ  16: 'number',       // Number
    28Ôćĺ  17: 'boolean',      // Boolean
    29Ôćĺ  18: 'array',        // Array
    30Ôćĺ  19: 'object',       // Object
    31Ôćĺ  20: 'key',          // Key
    32Ôćĺ  21: 'null',         // Null
    33Ôćĺ  22: 'enumMember',   // EnumMember
    34Ôćĺ  23: 'struct',       // Struct
    35Ôćĺ  24: 'event',        // Event
    36Ôćĺ  25: 'operator',     // Operator
    37Ôćĺ  26: 'typeParameter',// TypeParameter
    38Ôćĺ  27: 'parameter',    // Parameter
    39Ôćĺ  28: 'macro',        // Macro
    40Ôćĺ  29: 'typeAlias',    // TypeAlias
    41Ôćĺ  30: 'union',        // Union
    42Ôćĺ};
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ// SCIP Relationship types
    45Ôćĺconst RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_MAP = {
    46Ôćĺ  0: 'unspecified',
    47Ôćĺ  1: 'implements',
    48Ôćĺ  2: 'extends',
    49Ôćĺ  3: 'references',
    50Ôćĺ};
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ/**
    53Ôćĺ * Parse a SCIP symbol string into its components
    54Ôćĺ * Format: scheme package manager name version descriptor
    55Ôćĺ * @param {string} symbol - SCIP symbol string
    56Ôćĺ * @returns {Object} Parsed symbol components
    57Ôćĺ */
    58Ôćĺfunction parseSymbol(symbol) {
    59Ôćĺ  if (!symbol || symbol === '') {
    60Ôćĺ    return null;
    61Ôćĺ  }
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ  // SCIP symbols use the format: scheme package manager name version descriptor
    64Ôćĺ  // Example: scip-typescript npm typescript 4.9.5 src/lib/index.ts/
    65Ôćĺ  const parts = symbol.split(' ');
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ  if (parts.length < 4) {
    68Ôćĺ    // Local symbol format or invalid
    69Ôćĺ    return {
    70Ôćĺ      scheme: parts[0] || 'local',
    71Ôćĺ      packageManager: parts[1] || '',
    72Ôćĺ      packageName: parts[2] || '',
    73Ôćĺ      version: parts[3] || '',
    74Ôćĺ      descriptor: parts.slice(4).join(' ') || '',
    75Ôćĺ      isLocal: parts.length < 4,
    76Ôćĺ      raw: symbol,
    77Ôćĺ    };
    78Ôćĺ  }
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ  return {
    81Ôćĺ    scheme: parts[0],
    82Ôćĺ    packageManager: parts[1],
    83Ôćĺ    packageName: parts[2],
    84Ôćĺ    version: parts[3],
    85Ôćĺ    descriptor: parts.slice(4).join(' '),
    86Ôćĺ    isLocal: false,
    87Ôćĺ    raw: symbol,
    88Ôćĺ  };
    89Ôćĺ}
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ/**
    92Ôćĺ * Generate an internal symbol ID from SCIP symbol descriptor
    93Ôćĺ * @param {string} symbol - SCIP symbol string
    94Ôćĺ * @returns {string} Internal symbol ID
    95Ôćĺ */
    96Ôćĺfunction generateSymbolId(symbol) {
    97Ôćĺ  const parsed = parseSymbol(symbol);
    98Ôćĺ  if (!parsed) return null;
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ  // Create a hash-based ID for local symbols
   101Ôćĺ  if (parsed.isLocal) {
   102Ôćĺ    return `local:${hashString(symbol)}`;
   103Ôćĺ  }
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ  // For package symbols, use a structured ID
   106Ôćĺ  const descriptorHash = hashString(parsed.descriptor);
   107Ôćĺ  return `${parsed.scheme}:${parsed.packageManager}:${parsed.packageName}:${descriptorHash}`;
   108Ôćĺ}
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ/**
   111Ôćĺ * Simple string hash function
   112Ôćĺ * @param {string} str - Input string
   113Ôćĺ * @returns {string} Hash string
   114Ôćĺ */
   115Ôćĺfunction hashString(str) {
   116Ôćĺ  let hash = 0;
   117Ôćĺ  for (let i = 0; i < str.length; i++) {
   118Ôćĺ    const char = str.charCodeAt(i);
   119Ôćĺ    hash = ((hash << 5) - hash) + char;
   120Ôćĺ    hash = hash & hash;
   121Ôćĺ  }
   122Ôćĺ  return Math.abs(hash).toString(36);
   123Ôćĺ}
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺmodule.exports = {
   126Ôćĺ  SYMBOL_KIND_MAP,
   127Ôćĺ  RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_MAP,
   128Ôćĺ  parseSymbol,
   129Ôćĺ  generateSymbolId,
   130Ôćĺ  hashString,
   131Ôćĺ};
   132Ôćĺ

## Complete Codebase Analysis: SCIP Indexer Architecture

The codebase spans multiple worktrees within the `/Users/xin/auto-skills/` repository, with the current worktree (`worker-task-5-mnpeyzx1`) being a clean branch. The indexer-related code lives in sibling worktrees. Below is the full picture organized by component.

---

### 1. TypeScript Indexer (the reference implementation)

**File:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-4/src/indexers/typescript-indexer.ts`
(also at `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-4-mnpdixw5/src/indexers/typescript-indexer.ts`)

This is the canonical pattern for how an indexer works. Key interfaces:

```typescript
export interface IndexResult {
  files: string[];
  symbolCount: number;
  scipOutputPath: string;
}

export interface IndexOptions {
  projectRoot: string;
  output?: string;
  projectName?: string;
  // ...language-specific options
}

export interface ParserInput {
  scipOutputPath: string;
  projectRoot: string;
}

export type ParserFn = (input: ParserInput) => Promise<{ files: string[]; symbolCount: number }>;
```

The flow is:
1. **Validate** the project directory exists
2. **Run the SCIP tool** (e.g., `scip-typescript index --output=<path>`) via `execFileAsync`
3. **Parse the output** -- either via a custom `ParserFn` or via `extractScipMetadata()` which does raw binary scanning for file paths and symbol counts
4. **Return** an `IndexResult` with files, symbolCount, and scipOutputPath

Error handling includes detecting:
- Tool not found (ENOENT)
- Out of memory
- Missing dependencies
- Empty SCIP output

---

### 2. Go Indexer -- TypeScript Wrapper

**File:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-5/src/indexers/go-indexer.ts`

This wraps `scip-go` and parses its **JSON** output (unlike the TypeScript indexer which produces binary protobuf). Key differences:

- `scip-go` outputs `index.scip` in JSON format
- Defines its own `ScipSymbol` and `ParsedScipDocument` interfaces
- `parseScipSymbols()` extracts unique symbols from occurrences
- `findScipGo()` locates the binary in PATH
- `indexGoProject()` is the main entry point, taking `targetDir` and options

```typescript
export interface IndexResult {
  files: string[];
  symbolCount: number;
  errors: string[];
}

export async function indexGoProject(
  targetDir: string,
  options?: { scipGoPath?: string; outputDir?: string; timeoutMs?: number }
): Promise<IndexResult>
```

---

### 3. Go Indexer -- Native Go Implementation

**Directory:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-5-mnpdovxd/go-indexer/`

Files:
- **`/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-5-mnpdovxd/go-indexer/indexer.go`** -- Core indexer that runs `scip-go` as a subprocess
- **`/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-5-mnpdovxd/go-indexer/parser.go`** -- Parses SCIP **protobuf** output using `google.golang.org/protobuf/proto`
- **`/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-5-mnpdovxd/go-indexer/pipeline.go`** -- Orchestrates Run+Parse+Format
- **`/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-5-mnpdovxd/go-indexer/main.go`** -- CLI entry point with flags
- **`/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-5-mnpdovxd/go-indexer/indexer_test.go`** -- Unit tests

Key types:
```go
type Options struct {
    ModuleName, ModuleVersion, GoVersion string
    Timeout time.Duration
    NoQuotes bool
}

type Result struct {
    OutputPath   string
    IndexedFiles []string
    SymbolCount  int
    Duration     time.Duration
}
```

The Go indexer:
1. Validates `go.mod` exists
2. Finds `scip-go` in PATH
3. Runs `scip-go --output index.scip` with optional flags
4. Parses the binary protobuf output using the generated `Index` struct from `proto.Unmarshal`

### 4. SCIP Parser (TypeScript, protobuf-based)

**Directory:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-3-mnpd2iwb/scip-parser/`


Files:
- **`parser.ts`** -- Uses `protobufjs` to load `scip.proto` and decode binary SCIP indexes. Defines `SCIPParser` class, `SymbolRole` enum, `SymbolKind` enum
- **`symbol-mapper.ts`** -- `SymbolIdMapper` class that maps SCIP symbol strings to numeric IDs and parses symbol descriptors (handling `/`, `#`, `.`, `()`, `[]`, `:`, `!` separators)
- **`database.ts`** -- `SCIPDatabase` class using `better-sqlite3` with tables: `symbols`, `references`, `relationships`, `documents`, `metadata`
- **`index.ts`** -- Re-exports everything

### 5. SCIP Parser (JavaScript, hand-rolled protobuf)

**Directory:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-3-mnpedjqq/domain-tool-core/lib/`

Files:
- **`scip-parser.js`** -- Hand-written `ProtobufReader` class that reads varints, tags, length-delimited fields. Parses all SCIP message types: Index, Metadata, ToolInfo, Document, SymbolInformation, Occurrence, Relationship, Diagnostic, Documentation
- **`scip-utils.js`** -- `SYMBOL_KIND_MAP` (number-to-string), `RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_MAP`, `parseSymbol()` (parses SCIP symbol strings like `scip-typescript npm typescript 4.9.5 src/lib/index.ts/`), `generateSymbolId()` (hash-based ID generation)
- **`scip-indexer.js`** -- Orchestrates: loads SCIP file, processes documents/symbols/occurrences/relationships, populates SymbolDatabase
- **`symbol-database.js`** -- In-memory database using Maps: symbols, relationships, fileIndex, scipToInternal
- **`scip-indexer-cli.js`** -- CLI with commands: index, index-all, query, stats

### 6. Index Builder (unified, auto-detecting)

**File:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-4-mnpex558/src/indexer/builder.ts`

This is the highest-level abstraction that auto-detects project type:

```typescript
export type ProjectType = "typescript" | "go";

export function detectProjectType(projectPath: string): ProjectType | null {
  if (existsSync(join(projectPath, "tsconfig.json"))) return "typescript";
  if (existsSync(join(projectPath, "go.mod"))) return "go";
  return null;
}

export async function buildIndex(projectPath: string, projectType: ProjectType): Promise<IndexResult>
```

Flow:
1. Detect project type
2. Run appropriate SCIP tool (`scip-typescript` or `scip-go`)
3. Parse the JSON `index.scip` output
4. Write symbols to SQLite database

**CLI:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-4-mnpex558/src/cli/fg-index.ts`

### 7. Type Definitions (comprehensive)

**File:** `/Users/xin/auto-skills/.claude/worktrees/worker-task-b/src/types.ts`

Full SCIP type system including:
- `NodeType` enum (File, Module, Namespace, Class, Interface, Type, Enum, Function, Method, etc.)

- `Node` and `Edge` interfaces for the code graph

- `SymbolRole` (bit flags: Definition=1, Import=2, WriteAccess=4, ReadAccess=8, etc.)

`SCIPIndexImporter` class that:

- Batch inserts into a SQLite database
- Supports incremental imports (checks existing symbols)

### Key Patterns for Creating a Go Indexer

Based on all the existing implementations, the consistent patterns are:

1. **Interface-based**: Export `IndexResult`, `IndexOptions`, and a main `index*()` function
2. **Validation**: Check directory exists, check project marker file (`go.mod`)
3. **Tool detection**: Find the SCIP tool binary in PATH, give install instructions on ENOENT
4. **Execution**: Run the SCIP tool as a subprocess with appropriate flags and timeout
5. **Output parsing**: Parse the SCIP output file (either protobuf binary or JSON depending on tool)
6. **Error handling**: Graceful error messages for common failures (missing tool, OOM, missing deps)
7. **Cleanup**: Optional cleanup of temporary SCIP output files
8. **CLI**: `main.go` with `flag` package for configuration options

The native Go implementation in worktree `worker-task-5-mnpdovxd` is the most complete reference for a pure Go approach, using `google.golang.org/protobuf` for parsing and following the same structural patterns as the TypeScript indexer."}]}]}

Created `domain-tool-core/lib/go-indexer.js` (371 lines). Here's what it does:

**Pipeline**: `validateTargetDir` Ôćĺ `findScipGo` Ôćĺ `runScipGo` Ôćĺ `parseScipResult` Ôćĺ cleanup Ôćĺ return `{files, symbolCount, scipOutputPath}`

**Key components**:
- **`GoIndexerError`** ÔÇö custom error class with `cause` chain
- **`ProtobufReader`** + parser functions ÔÇö self-contained SCIP protobuf parser (no external dependency), parses `Index`, `Document`, `Occurrence`, `SymbolInformation`, `Metadata`, `ToolInfo`
- **`indexGoProject(targetDir, options?)`** ÔÇö main entry point
- **`findScipGo(customPath?)`** ÔÇö locates `scip-go` binary (custom path or PATH lookup)
- **`validateTargetDir(targetDir)`** ÔÇö checks directory exists, is a directory, and contains `go.mod`

**Error handling** covers: missing dir, no `go.mod`, binary not found (ENOENT), timeout, Go toolchain errors, crashes, empty/missing SCIP output, parse failures.

**Options**: `scipGoPath`, `output`, `timeoutMs` (default 120s), `keepOutput` (auto-cleans by default).

(no content)(no content)(no content)Build succeeded. The hook script is created at `src/hooks/mindkeeper-session-start.ts` and compiled to `dist/hooks/mindkeeper-session-start.js`.

Summary of what was implemented:

1. **Hook script for MindKeeper**: `src/hooks/mindkeeper-session-start.ts` - A standalone script that MindKeeper can call on session start

2. **Auto-detect project root from git**: `findGitRoot()` function walks up the directory tree looking for `.git` directory

3. **Incremental index update**: 
   - Maintains state in `.fg-index-state.json` tracking file mtimes and sizes
   - Uses `git ls-files` to get tracked files
   - Compares current file stats with cached state to detect changes
   - Only runs indexer when files have actually changed

4. **Logging to stderr**: All log messages go to stderr via `console.error()` with `[fg-index-hook]` prefix for easy filtering

{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"tool_rTaivcHHGsEIZ7LO1chq4P2r","type":"tool_result","content":"     1Ôćĺ# Auto-Skills Project Documentation
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺA comprehensive automation and deployment toolkit for domain configuration management and CI/CD processes.
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺ## Project Structure
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ```
     8Ôćĺauto-skills/
     9ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ bin/                    # Executable scripts accessible via PATH
    10ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ deploy             # Main deployment script (SCMP integration)
    11ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ push               # Git push helper with notifications
    12ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ lookup             # Domain/service lookup utility
    13ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ claude-provider    # Claude Code provider script
    14ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ figmamcp           # Figma integration script
    15ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ scmp-deploy/           # SCMP (Service Control Management Platform) tools
    16ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ README.md          # Deployment tool documentation
    17ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ scripts/
    18ÔćĺÔöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ scmp_cli.py    # Standalone SCMP CLI
    19ÔćĺÔöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ scmp_api.py    # HTTP API wrapper
    20ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ domain-tool-core/      # Central domain configuration engine
    21ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ index.js           # Main domain tool engine
    22ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ install.js         # Installation script for anchor system
    23ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ MAINTENANCE.md     # Maintenance manual
    24ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ package.json       # Dependencies and package info
    25ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ backup/                # Backup configurations and skills
    26ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ .skills/              # Skill configurations
    27Ôćĺ```
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ## Core Components
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ### 1. Domain Tool Core (`domain-tool-core/`)
    32Ôćĺ
    33ÔćĺThe central engine for domain configuration management that generates JSON configurations from Excel spreadsheets.
    34Ôćĺ
    35Ôćĺ**Key Features:**
    36Ôćĺ- Excel parsing and configuration generation
    37Ôćĺ- Ads mapping system with placeholder syntax
    38Ôćĺ- Dynamic ad slot resolution
    39Ôćĺ- Configuration template support
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ**Important Files:**
    42Ôćĺ- `index.js`: Main engine implementation
    43Ôćĺ- `install.js`: Sets up global anchor for cross-project access
    44Ôćĺ- `MAINTENANCE.md`: Complete maintenance and upgrade guide
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ**Configuration Syntax (v2.7.0+):**
    47Ôćĺ```javascript
    48ÔćĺadsMapping: {
    49Ôćĺ  adsense: {
    50Ôćĺ    'home_1': '${home_1}',     // New placeholder syntax
    51Ôćĺ    'list_top': '${list_1}'    // Left: output key, Right: Excel column
    52Ôćĺ  },
    53Ôćĺ  adx: {
    54Ôćĺ    'list_top': '${list_1}',
    55Ôćĺ    'categories[0]': '${list_2}', // Array element support
    56Ôćĺ    'categories[1]': '${list_3}'
    57Ôćĺ  }
    58Ôćĺ}
    59Ôćĺ```
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ### 2. SCMP Deploy Tools (`scmp-deploy/`)
    62Ôćĺ
    63ÔćĺStandalone command-line interface for deploying services through SCMP without opening a browser UI.
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ**Features:**
    66Ôćĺ- Authentication token management
    67Ôćĺ- Service discovery and pipeline selection
    68Ôćĺ- Parameter inference from current deployments
    69Ôćĺ- Automated deployment workflows
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ**Commands:**
    72Ôćĺ- `login`: Authenticate and save token
    73Ôćĺ- `service`: Search for services
    74Ôćĺ- `pipelines`: List pipelines for a service
    75Ôćĺ- `current`: Get current pipeline run parameters
    76Ôćĺ- `run`: Trigger pipeline run
    77Ôćĺ- `deploy`: One-shot deployment (recommended)
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ**Usage:**
    80Ôćĺ```bash
    81Ôćĺ# Login (first time setup)
    82Ôćĺpython3 scmp-deploy/scripts/scmp_cli.py login --share-id "<share_id>" --prompt-password
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ# Deploy service (interactive mode)
    85Ôćĺdeploy my-service-name
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ# Or direct CLI usage
    88Ôćĺpython3 scmp-deploy/scripts/scmp_cli.py deploy my-service-name --env prod
    89Ôćĺ```
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺ### 3. Global Scripts (`bin/`)
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ#### `deploy` Script
    94ÔćĺThe primary deployment command that wraps `scmp_cli.py`. Can infer service names from the current git repository.
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ**Features:**
    97Ôćĺ- Automatic service name detection from `.deploy-service` or `.deploy-name`
    98Ôćĺ- Interactive deployment with intelligent defaults
    99Ôćĺ- Daily token refresh
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ#### `push` Script
   102ÔćĺEnhanced git push workflow with notifications and version bumping.
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ**Features:**
   105Ôćĺ- Automatic version incrementation for branch names
   106Ôćĺ- Commit message prompting
   107Ôćĺ- Optional Feishu notifications
   108Ôćĺ- Pre/post hook support
   109Ôćĺ- Automatic `.deploy-service` file creation
   110Ôćĺ
   111Ôćĺ**Usage:**
   112Ôćĺ```bash
   113Ôćĺ# Standard push with commit message
   114Ôćĺpush -m "feat: add new functionality"
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ# Push without notifications
   117Ôćĺpush -m "fix: resolve issue" --no-notify
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ# Interactive push
   120Ôćĺpush  # Prompts for commit message
   121Ôćĺ```
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ#### `lookup` Script
   124ÔćĺDomain and service lookup utility for identifying associated services.
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ## Setup Instructions
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ### Prerequisites
   129Ôćĺ- Node.js >= 14.0.0
   130Ôćĺ- Python 3.x
   131Ôćĺ- Git
   132Ôćĺ
   133Ôćĺ### Initial Setup
   134Ôćĺ
   135Ôćĺ1. **Add bin directory to PATH:**
   136Ôćĺ```bash
   137Ôćĺecho 'export PATH="/Users/xin/auto-skills/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
   138Ôćĺsource ~/.zshrc
   139Ôćĺ```
   140Ôćĺ
   141Ôćĺ2. **Setup domain-tool-core anchor (if moving/renaming directory):**
   142Ôćĺ```bash
   143Ôćĺcd /path/to/auto-skills/domain-tool-core
   144Ôćĺnode install.js
   145Ôćĺ```
   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ3. **SCMP Deployment Setup:**
   148Ôćĺ```bash
   149Ôćĺ# Set your share ID
   150Ôćĺexport SCMP_SHARE_ID='<your-share-id>'
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ# Login to SCMP (token stored securely)
   153Ôćĺpython3 scmp-deploy/scripts/scmp_cli.py login --prompt-password
   154Ôćĺ```
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ4. **Configure Feishu notifications (optional):**
   157Ôćĺ```bash
   158Ôćĺexport FEISHU_WEBHOOK='<your-feishu-webhook-url>'
   159Ôćĺexport PUSH_NOTIFY=1  # Enable auto-notifications
   160Ôćĺ```
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ## Key Workflows
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ### 1. Service Deployment Workflow
   165Ôćĺ1. Navigate to your service repository
   166Ôćĺ2. Make your changes
   167Ôćĺ3. Use `push -m "commit message"` to commit and push with version bumping
   168Ôćĺ4. Use `deploy` (infers service name) or `deploy service-name` to deploy
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ### 2. Domain Configuration Workflow
   171Ôćĺ1. Prepare Excel spreadsheet with domain configurations
   172Ôćĺ2. Generate configuration using domain-tool-core
   173Ôćĺ3. Review `suggested-config.js` for correct mapping syntax
   174Ôćĺ4. Deploy updated configurations
   175Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ### 3. Development Best Practices
   177Ôćĺ- Use meaningful commit messages with conventional prefixes (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.)
   178Ôćĺ- Always review `suggested-config.js` when updating domain configurations
   179Ôćĺ- Maintain consistent branch naming with version numbers (e.g., `feature-1.2.3`)
   180Ôćĺ- Configure appropriate notification settings for team awareness
   181Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ## Security Notes
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ- Tokens are stored locally with 0600 permissions
   185Ôćĺ- Passwords are never stored in plain text
   186Ôćĺ- Use `--plain-password` flag carefully as it echoes passwords to terminal
   187Ôćĺ- Regular token refresh mechanism prevents stale authentication
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ## Troubleshooting
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ- If domain-tool-core path breaks after directory movement, run `node install.js` from the core directory
   192Ôćĺ- For deployment authentication issues, run the login command again
   193Ôćĺ- Check `.deploy-service` file exists in repositories for proper deployment integration
   194Ôćĺ- Review `scmp-deploy/README.md` for detailed troubleshooting of deployment issues
   195Ôćĺ
   196ÔćĺFor more detailed information on specific components, see the individual README files in each directory.
